articles.title: DBS launches AI wealth advisory platform for HNW clients
articles.summary: DBS Bank unveiled an AI-powered advisory platform targeting high-net-worth clients in Singapore and Hong Kong. The system uses LLMs to generate personalised portfolio recommendations.

articles.title: HSBC WPB reports record Asia AUM growth in Q1 2026
articles.summary: HSBC Wealth and Personal Banking reported a 14% year-on-year increase in assets under management across Asia Pacific, driven by strong inflows from mainland Chinese clients.

talking_points.headline: DBS AI wealth platform raises competitive bar for HSBC in HNW segment
talking_points.why_it_matters: As Head of Change Execution for APAC private banking, Adam needs visibility on competitor AI rollouts that could shift HNW client expectations before HSBC's own roadmap delivers.
bullets: Piyush Gupta, CEO of DBS confirmed the platform will expand to Hong Kong by Q3 2026.
The system integrates real-time market data with client goal modelling — a capability HSBC's internal teams are still piloting.
Early adoption metrics show 23% increase in product cross-sell within the first 60 days.

source_links.title: DBS launches AI wealth advisory platform

talking_points.headline: HSBC WPB Q1 AUM surge validates APAC wealth strategy under WPS
talking_points.why_it_matters: As COO & Global Head of Change Execution, Sirali will need to align operational capacity and change pipeline with the accelerated growth trajectory signalled by Q1 results.
bullets: 14% YoY AUM growth across APAC was driven primarily by mainland China inflows.
Noel Quinn, Group CEO of HSBC highlighted wealth as the primary growth engine for 2026.
Operational scaling will be critical — the COO function should anticipate increased demand for onboarding and servicing capacity.

source_links.title: HSBC WPB reports record Asia AUM growth
